reviews.review_msg: Rachel is good - her exams were fair, mostly, and assignments were very code-heavy. I didn't like her course structure that much, but you get to learn actually useful stuff. Lectures were easily skippable. However, she is very nice.

Prof. Altman is easily one of my favorite professors. She is so encouraging and genuinely cheers for her students' success. I felt totally comfortable asking questions and expressing my doubts, which really pushed me to think more deeply. I can't thank her enough for the mentorship that gave me the confidence to aim for grad school.
Only thing i would say, dont every try to get stat 851 on her course. you will regret it.

Rachel's lectures are among the most engaging I've ever had. Her assignments and reasonable in length. For exams & assignments, word choice is crucial, as marks can be determined by single words. Copying the wording on the slides which she posts is a good idea. Exams highly resemble the assignments. She was helpful during office hours and caring.

Solid professor for 350. Engaging lectures. Caring prof who makes an effort to get to know you. Lecture notes and materials are very organized and clear. Exams are fair and reflect the material taught. My interest in statistics increased after this class.

this prof is an easy fade for all hsci majors. she was hell in different stat courses both me and my friend took. The homework was relatively easy but worth almost nothing. The midterms and especially the final exam had incredibly difficult to understand problems which added another level of difficulty on top of actually solving the questions.
one of my fav stat profs !! amazing lectures with clear slides and good assignments. midterms match assignment and lecture topics. goated
Biased prof with sketchy understanding.

If you're able to self teach, this course with Rachel isn't bad. Assignment criteria was annoying and unclear and she does expect you to use identical wording from her own explanations. However she does really highlight what's important for exams, supplied review sessions for each exam, and allowed cheat sheets. Lowk she not too bad.
Received a D because I got under 50% on the final.... despite my overall grade being well above a passing 55%. Rough semester but even worse in this class

Horrible exams, grading schemes, and resources for help. Constantly makes mistakes for lecture notes/homework instructions and even exam preparations. No practice exams are given, but she still sends exam preparation instruction corrections at midnight, the night before the exam. You have to pass the final to pass the class. ClassaverageC Fall 2024

Class was a lot harder than it needed to be, lots of excess assignment questions compared to other stat profs when I spoke to a TA. If you like word memorization on top of studying and doing the coding homework assignments then this is the class for you, otherwise avoid. Exams you will be docked marks for not using the terminalogy from lectures
Professor is extremely disorganized with her notes with mistakes in the homework always
Avoid at all costs

The professor is nice and understanding; she is willing to help you succeed if you put in the effort and go to office hours. However, wording matters. The course is not necessarily hard, but you need to use specific wordings and make sure you understand the differences between the concepts, as it can get tricky.

Barely got through this class, my two friends who took the course both failed because they failed the final despite doing well on the midterms + assignments. I rarely every understood anything new from lecture as I always went through the notes before class and Rachel would just read off the slides word for word.

You screw up one exam you're lowkey cooked buddy, she shows no mercy with curving grades and marks super hard on the homework, aka you don't you the definition she's looking for. Avoid, take it with Becky as she's a lot better. This prof will singlehandedly lower your gpa and make you feel miserable after the class

even with office hours, I can't imagine how I did so well in the class. I believe it's due to the many hours I put doing practice quizzes on my own time. But based on other student reviews and my peers I can safely say, how does she even have a job? Her in class examples alone are hard to understand, don't forget about the many mistakes on exams

Before you ask about my grade, just know that this was the class average.... Absolutely horrendous prof who doesn't care about teaching other than showing up, reading the slides then sending 1am emails either about errors on the homework she assigned or complaints of students collaborating on homework assignments. Avoid her if possible

The course material is not hard. However, to get good grades, you should follow her wording in the slides EXACTLY. Expecting to memorize a lot of concepts. Exams focus more on interpretation rather than math/proof. She is very willing to help during office hours. Personally, I think she is good if you spend enough time on this course.
she grades soooo unnecessarily difficult that it makes it extremely difficult to get a good grade. lectures were very confusing but explains things easier in office hours

Exams very long and grade on very specific terminology doesn't matter if you actually understand material. Lots of minor errors on the exam will add up and tank your grade very quickly. If you have the right terminology but wrong phrasing is used answer is wrong, was confusing because they don't tell you that in person. No real feedback was given.

Most people complained about her but her class was quite straight forward. The assignments were pretty much an exact replica of the type of questions on exams. I definitely recommend getting the text book and doing the practice questions there because they are much harder than exam questions so they will prepare you.

Worst prof of all time She constantly makes mistakes in lectures The content is not too bad if you read the slides and make sure you understand it. The exams didn't make sense at all (written) They're picky with word choice too The assignments are irrelevant to lectures. She fixes her mistakes at the last min. Her TAs don't do anything. Avoid her!

Professor Altman graded exams and assignments extremely hard, marks off if you don't use a specific word. On top, she never mentions anything regarding assignments or how to do them in lectures. Her Grading scheme was weird as you needed a 64% to get a C. No wonder the class average was a C with many getting C- or even failing Avoid her if possible
worst prof of my life

Avoid this prof. made a lot of mistakes in her lectures and was never sure what to answer when she was questioned. For the final exam we needed to get a 50% on the final to pass this course and if you didn't you get a Fail in the course which is horrible. The online prof is much better Gamage Perra, his class average was B- and for this it was C.

Class material was easy to understand but the prof herself was somewhat infuriating. many times she would email about some kind of correction made on the assignments or lectures like 1-2 days before the due date/exam date. midterms we were allowed a double sided sheet of paper but shes very picky about wording for the interpretations

Terrible prof often makes mistakes in her lecture slides and will get an email at like 1 am the day before an exam that she corrected. Very rude went to office hours couple times but did not explain anything and in a passive aggressive rude tone.

Dr Altman often makes mistakes in lectures, assignments and exams that are corrected at the last minute, often contributing to students' confusion. In lecture she often contradicted herself and answered students' questions with long tangents that did not answer the initial question. Learn with friends based on assignment contents and key formulas.

Gives indirect answers and is poor at explaining concepts. Made so many mistakes in slides, assignments, and exams. Marking is so harsh and takes off so many minor points which will tank your assignment grades. I never found any resources outside of class to be helpful, either. The final was pass or fail. Always let us in quite late to exams, too.
Not sure how STAT 201 has a C average final grade. So unnecessarily harsh for what?

Ummm. She was fine during lectures, seems to care. Her slides are informative and easy to read through. Tests and assignments were fair. The course was not curved though. The average was a C. I seriously wonder how many people failed lol..

The material was easy to grasp, but she was unapproachable and rambling. Her lectures were awkward and uninteresting. I often felt bad for people asking questions in class because she responded very rudely. She closed discussion boards and shut down questions despite claiming to encourage them. Professor Altman was uncaring and bland. Avoid.

DO NOT TAKE HER CLASS!! She always brings up an error in the homework or in her slides the night before due dates and tests. She avoids fully answering questions. She is a tough marker, and she provides very vague feedback on why the answers are wrong. She is not an engaging teacher, as everyone leaves right after taking the participation quiz.

Horrible prof. She only reads off of the slides, her classes are insanely boring, and she makes attendance mandatory, as you need a code to access the participation quizzes.  Overall, this class is so boring that everyone leaves after the participation quizzes are done.

One of the worst classes I have ever taken at SFU. Her slides are always incorrect and she can never fully answer a student's question in class and always tells them to "think about it." AVOID IF NECESSARY, she is an extremely tough marker and will not tell you what you did wrong

She was very approachable, was willing to have conversations with students and very very humble. She always made sure students understood her topics and took time out of her day to ensure this. I really enjoyed taking STAT 201 with her, given that it is a conventionally "difficult" course for students in the life sciences.

Terrible experience, contradicts herself, I am not sure she knows what she is doing.  Unapproachable, had to redact many exam and assignment questions because she made errors or everyone got them wrong because of her poor lectures and lack of communication.

disengaged, often missing discussions and not responding. Though she claims to be strict, it's likely a cover for poor communication. Her materials are hard to follow, and students often turn to outside sources. She seems knowledgeable but lacks the passion or ability to teach effectively, making her class uninspiring and unsupportive.

Says that she gives you all the code needed for R Studio but when students posted questions asking about the code because they weren't sure how to use it she shut down the discussion board because she took it personally

so unbelievable, avoid as much as possible. average for all exams was 50% because she didnt give us a formula sheet and didnt allow cheat sheets so ur on ur own for a STATISTICS class. not sure how she expected us to memorize everything. Wish i dropped out when I had the chance.

Absolutely awful, avoid her at all costs. Half the class failed the first midterm (which had multiple choice), so she decided to make the 2nd midterm and final fully written. Which understandably half the class failed the 2nd midterm and final exam. Not scaled or curved, and no cheat sheet or formula sheets given for exams.

For 305: would recommend any other prof other than her for this class. No cheat sheets allowed unlike every other stats class, given 3 formulas to help for the entire course. Class average is borderline 50%. And if you don't pass the final, you fail the course. Wish I dropped out before the deadline and taken a different prof.

This is for stat 305 but it was not an option. Horrible. No formula sheet during exams, no cheat sheet, expected to memorize EVERYTHING. exam is fully written no MC. got mad at us when the midterms average was extremely low even though she is obviously the reason its so low. expecting us to memorize equations on top of definitions is horrible
Awful, Awful. Avoid Avoid

Rachel is one of the best teachers I have seen so far. She exactly knows how to explain a new concept so that students do not get confused. She was always quickly available to answer student questions. we had an exam or an assignment almost every two weeks throughout the term, which was really helpful for learning the course materials. Perfect!!

STAT302 Final average grade is C+, midterm average is around 50% to 60%,I took six upper courses in these two semesters and got five A levels, but 302 was a B.If you want a high GPA, Rachel is definitely not your choice.

Amazing professor. Super accessible outside class; the discussion forms are extremely helpful and she replies within the day. Does a great job re-explaining concepts if they're unclear. Bad ratings are likely from those that don't put in the effort to understand the material. I would recommend a very basic knowledge of R before taking this course.
Speechless course.

The major questions on the final were only ever taught on a theoretical level before. So, many assumptions and improvisations had to be made which was confusing. Be prepared to devote lots and lots of time to study for this course with Rachel. Could have taught the course better by showing more a holistic overview of some statistical methods.

I don't know why she gets so many bad ratings. She's nice, seems to care about students succeeding, and very fair about marking. I think her lectures were reasonably good. I managed to learn everything only from them. The TAs were terrible at marking, so you had to appeal every single assignment, but that wasn't Rachel's fault.
If you want to do well in this course, you shouldn't take it with her. She is a good person, but "Not a good prof".

She is very passionate about statistics, but she doesn't have much teaching ability so lectures are somewhat useless. Lecture slides weren't the best too, although exams were extremely fair considering the course material. Assignments due at 5pm which was strange. Learned a lot so would recommend her for STAT270. Very active on canvas discussion.
A solid professor. The lectures give you the tools you need to complete the assignments and for the tests.

I really liked Rachel. Very helpful and caring, lectures were pretty clear even though the class was quite challenging. Once you finish viewing all the lecture videos, the assignments and the exams are very doable!

Prof was very nice, assignments were straightforward and she was always willing to answer any questions. Final was straightforward as well. Overall I enjoyed this class and would take another class with her
Awful awful awful
Hard course. Made even harder by terrible instructor. And seems to be a common theme, but pretty snide emails. Avoid at all costs
Very condescending emails, total disregard to students. One of my most horrible experiences in SFU.
Sad I can't give 0 stars. Worst class I've ever taken, will never take STAT again
tough grader, terrible lecturer (super boring), condescending
Wack. This course will wreck your GPA
Don't. Take.
Rachel is very nice and helpful. Always quickly available to answer student questions and tests on material that is very related to the slides/what is taught in class.
Awful professor for an awful course. Watch lectures at 2x speed. If you're in CS, take BUS232 instead and save yourself the trouble. Oh, and thanks for the snide e-mails.

She can make mistakes sometimes like having questions that are too hard, but she will correct them like reducing the total amount of points on a midterm. She might also add questions to assignments but she'll give you an extra week. Her lecture notes are helpful and readable, and her exams are fairly straightforward. Review Calc 2 for integration.

My peers told me STAT 270 would be a cumbersome course. Fortunately, I had the exact opposite experience taking it with Professor Altman :). Her lectures are extremely helpful, and her exams & assignments are fair. Definitely the kindest and most helpful instructor for STAT 270!
